title: 10 possible side effects of Symphytum officinale (Comfrey) if used improperly
intro: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, has been reported to have potential health side effects if used improperly. Prolonged use or ingestion of comfrey can cause damage to the liver, as it contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be toxic to the liver. Additionally, topical use of comfrey on broken skin can lead to skin irritation and inflammation. Pregnant women are advised to avoid using comfrey, as it may stimulate the uterus and lead to premature labor or miscarriage. Furthermore, excessive intake of comfrey can result in nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Therefore, it is essential to use comfrey with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
